key_insight: Laser-induced acoustic spectrograms, classified with a CNN, can non-contactly distinguish defect-free from defective laser-welded screws with high accuracy.
engineering_relevance: This enables scalable post-process quality assurance for laser welding without relying on traditional contact-based NDT methods.
method: Record laser-induced acoustic responses with a membrane-free optical microphone
Train a CNN and compare it with classical ML baselines using engineered features
evidence_signal: The optimized CNN reached 93.5% accuracy at the segment level and 92.9% at the specimen level, outperforming baseline ML approaches.
watch_item: Performance depends strongly on spectrogram resolution and network depth, so robustness across materials, geometries, and production conditions still needs validation.
paper_title: CNN-based acoustic spectrogram analysis for quality assessment of laser-welded screws
